revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ichisuka Kofun Cluster]][[fr:Groupe de kofun d'Ichisuka]][[ja:一須賀古墳群]]
{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns in Kanan, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ichisuka Kofun Cluster | native_name =一須賀古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =一須賀古墳群E-1号墳.JPG|image= File:一須賀古墳群E-1号墳.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ichisuka Kofun Cluster, Kofun E-1 | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ichisuka Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|30|8.5|N|135|38|46.0|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|34.502361|N|135.646111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|qid=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|qid=Q2305508|lt=Kanan, Osaka|1=Kanan, Osaka}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Kanan|qid=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}}; {{ill|Taishi|qid=Q1347173|1=Taishi, Osaka}}| region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park, Museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|8.5|N|135|38|46.0|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ichisuka Kofun Cluster'''|一須賀古墳群|Ichisuka Kofun gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, scattered on the hills from Hamuro, in the town of {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|qid=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}} to Ichisuka and Higashiyama, in the town of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|qid=Q2305508|lt=Kanan|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1994.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139047 |title=一須賀古墳群|trans-title= Ichisuka Kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ichisuka Kofun Cluster consists of 262 circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumuli, in 23 groups of several to dozens per group. Most of the tumuli are small with a diameters of 15 to 20 meters, and contain a horizontal hole type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], except for a few that have a wooden coffin direct burial. Two or four stone sarcophagi or wooden coffins are placed in the burial chamber, and miniature rice cookers such as a ''{{ill|Kamado|en|Kamado|qid=Q12177411|lt=Kamado|1=Kamado}}'' is placed as one of the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. Other grave goods included fragments of gold and bronze shoes, jewelry fragments, and the head of an iron sword, horse fittings and beads, along with quantities of Korean-style pottery shards. Judging from the [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, it is thought that the construction of these tumuli started in the first half of the 6th century and ended from the beginning to the first half of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the miniature rice cooker as a ritual implement, it is estimated that ''{{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|qid=Q11561926|lt=Toraijin|1=Toraijin}}'', immigrants from the Korean kingdoms of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|qid=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}} and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] were involved in the construction of this tumulus group. The area where the Ichisuka Kofun Cluster is located, is also called "Chikatsu Asuka", and was a location where many Baekje migrants settled around the 6th century. The [[Soga clan]], who emerged as a new force in the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} at the same time, had a close relationship with this region and also with Baekje kingdom. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Excavations were conducted in 1966; however, approximately 30 ''kofun'' on the western side of the site were destroyed from 1965 to 1974 due to the residential land development work. Recognizing the importance of the tumulus group, the Osaka Prefectural government acquired 29 hectares of the most densely populated area of the site for preservation. Currently, the area is opened to the public as the {{nihongo|'''Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-ga-oka Park'''|大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘|}}, with 32 of the 102 tumuli that have been preserved on a walking path. Then park is located a five-minute walk from the "Hannan Neopolis" on the Kongo Bus from {{ill|Kishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kishi Station (Osaka)|qid=Q6416576|lt=Kishi Station|1=Kishi Station (Osaka)}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nagano Line|Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 一須賀古墳群B-4号墳.JPG|B-4 Kofun 一須賀古墳群B-7号墳.JPG|B-7 Kofun 一須賀古墳群B-9号墳復元家形石棺.JPG|B-9 Kofun 一須賀古墳群E-1号墳.JPG|E-1 Kofun 一須賀古墳群I-7号墳.JPG|I-7 Kofun 近つ飛鳥博物館.JPG|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Ichisuka Kofun Group}} *[https://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko/rekishi_shiseki/ichisukakofungun.html Taishi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/itisuga.html Osaka Prefectural Government] {{in lang|ja}}
